Alexandre United States Superior Twin Room 1 night · March 2026 Solo traveller Reviewed: 17 April 2026 Very nice and clean hotel facility. Disappointing breakfast with multiple options. Scored 7.0 7.0 Room and facilities were very clean and well-maintained. Security was very good with working dog patrols and security gate access. Bathroom/shower was clean with good quality towels. Breakfast was a great disappointment albeit with a good variety of options. Coffee machine was broken and a coffee pot was brought out to provide no options for coffee. The eggs-to-order grill was unattended. After 10 minutes I asked the restaurant attendant for assistance and it took another five minutes for the cook to arrive, by which time so many others had lined up to order I had to wait longer (having previously been first in line), and was provided an overcooked plane egg omelet despite ordering a vegetarian omelet with all the vegetables available (and the cook even asked to confirm “omelet with everything but no meat”). What was provided as orange juice was not orange juice but a sweetened orange-flavored drink made from a powder mix. Disappointing. When I mentioned this upon checkout, I was assured the breakfast — which I did not eat — would not be charged and I would be provided a free breakfast upon my return visit days later… which I was not provided. Some of the staff was very courteous while others were unhelpful and seemed annoyed with having to interact with guests. The hotel is located on an extremely busy multi-lane road and intersection by the airport property but 1.6 km from terminals 2 and 3 that precludes walking safely. There was nothing in the neighborhood worth walking to or to see. Hotel response: Dear Valued Guest,  Thank you for sharing your feedback... Continue reading Helpful Not helpful

Antony Switzerland King Guest room with Nile View 1 night · April 2026 Couple Reviewed: 24 April 2026 Very good Scored 8.0 8.0 The Hilton is more or less what you would expect from a 5 star hotel and and of all the hotels we stayed at in Egypt, the Hilton had the most outstanding breakfast. Everything you could want was there and available. Location-wise the hotel is quite centrally-located so if you want to see Coptic Cairo and the Citadel (you do) this is a reasonable location. The Pyramids and the Grand Egyptian Museum are a little further away. We stayed for one night only as we were on our way home after a trip further south, but I believe you will have a very comfortable stay if you stay here. The one thing we didn't like was the airport transfer options. The average taxi price is around 30 dollars from the airport to central-ish Cairo - we paid this to transfer to Giza. However the meet and assist service costs substantially more (almost the price of another night's stay). We did not take this option, but we are unclear what it would offer (we simply wanted to get to the hotel without the taxi scrum at the airport). Similarly the return journey to the airport cost 45 dollars, and while the car was indeed very nice we did not need such an expensive option. I would suggest the hotel look into providing a range of options - a reasonably priced reputable service would be more than sufficient and would help travellers avoid the taxi harassment at Cairo Airport, while those who want a fancier car can take it.
